name: template
description: Sandboxed templating languages for AQL: render text templates against a data context through one common interface, exposed as the single `Template` namespace. The mustache engine is implemented; handlebars/liquid/jinja share the same pipeline and are reserved.

conventions.import: import "./template.aql"  — the library imports its own aql:* deps (aql:parse, aql:parselang, aql:string-util, aql:vm); callers need not
conventions.call: receiver-first, arguments forward (preferred): `receiver Template.verb arg...`. Group in parens to use the result as a value, e.g. `(tpl Template.render ctx)`.
conventions.construct: build compiled templates ONLY via Template.compile; treat Compiled fields as read-only
conventions.pipeline: each render is parse (aql:parse grammar) -> compile (to an AQL program of custom tpl_* words) -> run (aql:vm sub-engine under a totally restricted policy with every capability scope uninstalled), so a template can never do I/O or escape the sandbox
conventions.import_resolution: relative to the working directory the script is run from, not the importing file
conventions.errors: failures raise coded errors caught with `do [...] error [...]`; read e.code / e.message via `e get "code"` (a QUOTED string key — on this build `get` evaluates its key argument). Codes: bad_input (compile/render args missing or wrong type), unknown_engine (engine not implemented), template_syntax (malformed/unbalanced template; a truly unterminated tag surfaces as parse_syntax_error from the parser).
conventions.escaping: {{var}} is HTML-escaped (& < > "); {{{var}}} and {{& var}} are raw

Compiled.note: construct only via Template.compile; fields are read-only. `engine` is the engine name; `program` is the generated AQL source run in the sandbox.

compile.note: Parse + compile a template once. Raises bad_input on missing/mistyped engine or source; unknown_engine for an unimplemented engine.

render.call: compiled Template.render context  |  {engine, source, context} Template.render
render.note: Two forms: render a Compiled against a context, or the one-shot options map (compile + render). Runs in the sandbox; raises template_syntax on a malformed template.
